skill.name: baoyu-image-gen
skill.description: Generate images through OpenAI or Google image APIs with prompt files, reference images, aspect ratio controls, and quality presets.
skill.summary: Generate API-backed images with OpenAI or Google from prompts, files, and optional references.
skill.author: jimliu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many dangerous patterns, but most external-command and weak-crypto hits are false positives from documentation, template strings, model names, or normal dynamic imports. The confirmed risks are high because the skill reads local files and environment files, accepts custom provider base URLs, and sends API keys and prompt/reference content over the network. No prompt injection text or confirmed malicious intent was found, so this is not blocked as critical.

high_findings.title: Custom Provider Base URL Can Receive API Keys
high_findings.description: The skill supports OPENAI_BASE_URL and GOOGLE_BASE_URL, then sends Authorization or x-goog-api-key headers to the selected endpoint. If an untrusted project or environment controls these values, provider API keys can be sent to a non-provider server.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly reads base URL environment variables and uses them in fetch calls that include API key headers. This is a clear credential exposure risk when configuration is attacker-controlled.

high_findings.title: Project Environment File Can Influence Secret-Bearing Requests
high_findings.description: The loader reads <cwd>/.baoyu-skills/.env and copies values into process.env when a variable is unset. In an untrusted project, this can steer provider selection, model settings, or custom endpoints used with existing API keys.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file load path and environment assignment are explicit. The impact depends on execution in an untrusted directory, so confidence is high but not absolute malicious intent.

high_findings.title: Local File Contents Can Be Sent To External Image Providers
high_findings.description: The --promptfiles and --ref options read caller-supplied local files. Prompt text and reference image bytes are then included in Google or OpenAI provider requests, which can disclose local file contents if the agent supplies sensitive paths.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code plainly reads local files and sends derived prompt or inline image data to network APIs. The risk requires unsafe path choices by the agent or user.

medium_findings.title: Arbitrary Output Path Write
medium_findings.description: The --image argument is resolved to an absolute path, parent directories are created, and image bytes are written there. This is expected for an image tool but can overwrite files if the caller chooses a sensitive path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path resolution and write are direct. It is legitimate output behavior, so the risk is moderate rather than inherently malicious.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported shell execution hits in SKILL.md are usage examples, and the reported hits in TypeScript are template literals or console output. I found no child_process, shell spawn, eval, or backtick command execution in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code is documentation or string interpolation, not an execution sink. The reviewed files do not contain shell execution APIs.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Is Provider Selection, Not Arbitrary Code Loading
low_findings.description: The dynamic imports select between two fixed local provider modules after provider validation. This does not allow a user-controlled module path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The provider value is constrained to google or openai before import. The import targets are fixed strings.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Model-Name False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections correspond to text such as dall-e-3, imagen-3.0, or markdown table syntax. No hashing or encryption implementation was found in the reviewed source.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are model identifiers and documentation, not cryptographic operations. This strongly supports a false positive assessment.

dangerous_patterns.title: Secret Header Sent To Configurable Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: Provider API keys are attached to requests whose base URL can be supplied through environment variables.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The base URL and API key header are present in the same request construction path.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Read Followed By Network Submission
dangerous_patterns.description: Prompt files and Google reference images are read from disk before generation requests are sent to external providers.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file read and subsequent network request flow are explicit in the code. The sensitivity depends on selected file paths.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Environment File Loading
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill reads hidden .baoyu-skills/.env files from the home directory and current project directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hidden env file paths are documented and implemented. This is legitimate configuration but carries risk in untrusted projects.

content.user_title: Generate API Images with OpenAI or Google
content.value_statement: Image workflows often need repeatable API generation from prompts, files, and reference images. This skill provides a command-driven generator with provider selection, aspect ratios, quality presets, and saved output files.
actual_capabilities: Generates images through OpenAI or Google provider APIs.
Accepts inline prompts, prompt files, or standard input as prompt sources.
Supports aspect ratio, size, image size, quality, model, and provider options.
Reads Google reference images and sends them as multimodal inline data.
Saves generated image bytes to a requested local output path.
Can return a short JSON summary with saved path, provider, model, and prompt preview.
limitations: Requires valid OpenAI, Google, or Gemini API keys before generation can work.
Reference images are only implemented for Google multimodal models.
OpenAI generation ignores reference images and maps aspect ratios to supported sizes.
The tool writes one output file path even when some providers may support multiple images.

use_cases.title: Create Product Mockups
use_cases.description: Generate concept images with fixed aspect ratios for product pages, social posts, or campaign drafts.

use_cases.title: Draft Visual Assets From Documents
use_cases.description: Read prompt files from a project and produce consistent illustrations for articles, slides, or reports.

use_cases.title: Automate Batch Image Requests
use_cases.description: Run repeatable provider-backed image generation from scripts with explicit model, provider, and output settings.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a clean product hero scene for a reusable water bottle. Save it as hero.png.

prompt_templates.title: Set Aspect Ratio and Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 16:9 high quality image for a webinar cover about secure AI workflows. Save it as webinar-cover.png.

prompt_templates.title: Use Prompt Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image using the prompt files brand-style.md and campaign-brief.md. Save the result as campaign-visual.png.

prompt_templates.title: Use a Reference Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use reference.png as visual guidance and generate a matching 4:3 illustration with a softer color palette. Save it as revised-concept.png.

output_examples.input: Generate a square image of a modern desk setup with warm lighting.
output_examples.output: The skill saves a generated PNG file at the requested path and prints that path for later use.

output_examples.input: Create a 16:9 cover image from two prompt files.
output_examples.output: The skill combines the prompt file text, calls the selected provider, and writes a landscape image file.

output_examples.input: Generate with a Google reference image and request JSON output.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a concise summary containing the saved image path, selected provider, selected model, and a prompt preview.

best_practices: Use explicit provider and model options when reproducibility matters.
Review prompt files and reference image paths before sending them to external providers.
Set API keys through trusted environment configuration, not untrusted project files.
anti_patterns: Do not run the skill in untrusted repositories that contain unknown .baoyu-skills/.env files.
Do not pass sensitive documents or private images as prompt or reference inputs.
Do not set custom provider base URLs unless the endpoint is trusted.

faq.question: Which providers does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports OpenAI image generation and Google Gemini or Imagen image generation through API calls.

faq.question: Does it need API keys?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, or GEMINI_API_KEY depending on the selected provider.

faq.question: Can it use reference images?
faq.answer: Yes, but reference images are only sent to Google multimodal models. OpenAI reference images are ignored.

faq.question: Can it control aspect ratio?
faq.answer: Yes. It accepts aspect ratio settings and maps them to provider-specific image parameters or prompt text.

faq.question: Where are generated images saved?
faq.answer: Images are written to the local path supplied with the image option. Missing parent directories are created.

faq.question: What is the main security concern?
faq.answer: The skill can read local files and send data to configurable API endpoints, so users must avoid untrusted paths and endpoints.
